12. As chengxin relates to all social members, shehui xinyong tixi in this context can be translated as
               “system of social credit.”
                     13. See Planning Outline, Part II (B) and II (C).
                     14. Guidelines on Accelerating the Construction of the Social Credit System and Establishing a New
               Credit-Based Regulatory Mechanism (Guidelines on Credit-Based Regulation), 2019, Item [8], Table 1.
                     15. Shanghai Municipal Regulations on Social Credit [上海市社会信用条例] (Shanghai
               Regulations), June 23, 2017 (adopted by the Standing Committee of Shanghai Municipal People’s
               Congress; effective October 1, 2017), Art. 2. The legislative drafters declared that the deﬁnition
               demonstrates the dual nature of social credit—namely, “market economy [regulation] and social
               governance” (Pan 2016).
